title: `podman machine set --cpus` accepts invalid CPU counts -> VM fails to start (krunkit abort / vfkit exit 1)
body:
### Issue Description

The `podman machine set --cpus` accepts very large values (e.g. `200`) without validating them against the host. After that, `podman machine start` fails with low-level errors instead of a clear validation message. By contrast, podman machine set `--memory` rejects values above total system memory with an explicit error.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman machine init test2`
2. `podman machine set --cpus 200 test2` -> no error
3. `podman machine start test2` -> fails


### Describe the results you received

`libkrun`: `Error: krunkit was terminated by signal: abort trap`
`vfkit`: `Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 1`

### Describe the results you expected

Reject `--cpus` values that exceed a sensible upper bound (e.g. logical CPU count on the host, and a documented cap (libkrun max: 16 cpus), with a message similar in spirit to memory validation.

Something like this but for `--cpus`:
```
podman machine set --memory 9999999999 test
Error: requested amount of memory (9999999999 MB) greater than total system memory (36864 MB)
```
